entry.title: OpenAI’s Groundbreaking 🔥$6.6B Investment and Latest Real-Time API
entry.content:
Credits: OpenAI

OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, is still leading in AI tech. After raising $6.6 billion in funding and reaching a valuation of $157 billion, they’ve just rolled out a new tool — the Real-time API Beta something that we would like to see in SAP infrastructure as well.

This move, combined with their financial growth, keeps OpenAI at the cutting edge of AI and shows they’re not slowing down anytime soon making it even more challenging for SAP to compete on this cutting edge technology. As showed on recent SAP TechEd it seems that SAP is all in with an AI and try to chase OpenAI releasing own LLM model for ABAP.

The $6.6 Billion Funding Milestone

OpenAI’s latest funding round is the largest in venture capital history. Led by Thrive Capital and supported by major tech players like Microsoft, Nvidia, and SoftBank, this investment brings the company’s total funding to $17.9 billion. Microsoft, a close partner, invested nearly $1 billion, while Nvidia and SoftBank contributed $100 million and $500 million, respectively.

This enormous influx of capital is crucial for OpenAI as it continues to scale and improve its AI models. The company plans to use the funds to:

  • Enhance AI research: OpenAI will focus on making new models more advanced and capable of solving increasingly complex problems.
  • Expand its AI infrastructure: OpenAI is exploring the possibility of developing its own computer chips to reduce its reliance on external providers like Nvidia. Interestingly, despite this, Nvidia is one of the major investors in OpenAI’s recent funding round. This highlights how intertwined partnerships in the tech industry can be, as Nvidia both supports OpenAI’s growth while also facing future competition in hardware development.

  • Attract top talent: With the recent departures of key leaders like CTO Mira Murati and research chief Bob McGrew, OpenAI needs more than just new hires — it requires exceptional talent to fill these gaps and drive its ambitious projects forward.

The Real-Time API Beta

OpenAI’s recent launch of the Real-time API Beta is one of its most exciting advancements yet. This new tool enables developers to create low-latency, multi-modal conversational experiences, meaning it supports both text and audio as input and output in real-time. Such feature can be integrated into the SAP BTP so hopefully we will see it together with ABAP AI as promissed on SAP TechEd 2024 in first quarter of 2025.

Credits: OpenAI

Some standout features of the Real-time API include:

  • Native speech-to-speech capability: Unlike older models that convert speech to text before processing, the Real-time API handles speech directly, leading to faster responses and more nuanced conversations.

  • Natural, steerable voices: The voices generated by the API can laugh, whisper, and even change their tone based on the context, making interactions feel more human and lifelike.

  • Simultaneous multimodal output: Text can be used for moderation or reference while the audio output is delivered faster than real time, ensuring smooth and stable playback.

This API is perfect for developers building voice assistants, real-time translation tools, and customer support bots, enabling them to create more engaging, human-like conversations with minimal delay. It also has potential applications in industries like entertainment, healthcare, and education, where real-time, multi-modal communication is key.

What’s Next for OpenAI?

With this record-breaking funding and the launch of the Real-time API, OpenAI is set to continue leading the AI revolution. The company plans to further expand its AI models and create more powerful tools. It also aims to remain competitive as new players, like Anthropic and xAI, enter the field.

The company has already set ambitious revenue targets, projecting $100 billion in sales by 2029. And with its new API, OpenAI is unlocking fresh possibilities in how people interact with technology, promising faster, more natural, and more immersive AI experiences.

How SAP will respond to such a huge investment and recent speech API, what do you think?

#OpenAI #VC #investment #SamAltman #AI #STT #TTS #LLM

Would like to connect?

Liked what you read and would like to connect? Let me know in chat and join my LinkedIn

My brands: Instago Inc. (AI Agents), Haergi Ltd. (SAP) & Haergi Sp. z o.o. (SAP)

entry.title: To add an additional field for "Supplier" in Find Maintenance Order and Operation App (F2175)
entry.content:
This app does have a filter "Supplier" in it which refer to the Vendor field in the external data tab, but it does not consider the "Supplier" field which is maintained for the Operation in Services Tab (When the enhanced procurement is set ON), where one operation can have more than one supplier assigned to it.

To achieve the filtering based on the supplier assigned to operations in Services Tab, I want to have an additional field added in this app. From a technical perspective is this a complex task or a simple task where we need to just map OData services to the newly added field.

The end goal is to have a work pack created based on suppliers grouping. And the first step to that is to have display the suppliers assigned to an operation.

entry.title: Integrated Financial Planning - Mathematical Foundation
entry.content:
"Integrated Financial Planning for SAP S/4HANA and S/4HANA Cloud" is the content package for SAP Analytics Cloud that covers the complete area of financial planning. The blog post Integrated Financial Planning - An Overview provides a single entry point for all information (documentation, presentations, videos, blog posts) around this topic.

With this blog post now, I want to outline the mathematical background of this content package and help to create a deep understanding of the ideas behind it. "Integrated Financial Planning" is not only called "integrated" because it integrates with SAP S/4HANA, SAP IBP and other business planning related content packages. It is integrated in itself because it brings together several SAC models (OPEX planning, Product cost planning, profitability planning, CAPEX planning, financial statement planning) in a consistent way. As an example,  the costs within the different models sum up into the respective cost lines in the aggregated P&L model.

Integrated Financial Planning is based on input-output relations in a network of objects: A unit of a finished product needs a certain unit of semi-finished goods, a unit of a semi-finished good needs some machine hours from a production cost center to be produced, for each machine hour the production cost center needs some kWh electricity from a supporting cost center and so on. In the same way as quantity flow backwards in this network of objects, the costs flow in the opposite direction. 

Linear algebra with matrix and vector calculations is a suitable instrument to describe and calculate these kinds of network models. In the attached document I will demonstrate this in detail and provide a homogeneous and consistent framework as the basis for financial planning. This background information also could help customers, partners and consultants to implement and potentially adapt the content package in a consistent way.

Hello @David_Chaviano & Digital Skill Initiative Team,

I am a woman, one of the people looking to switch their careers mid-life, and financial lack to afford a certification attempt.

I would greatly appreciate it if you could give me a chance for a free attempt at obtaining my certification. It would be very beneficial for my future career path.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I am looking forward for your support.

Best Regards,

entry.title: Send SAP ALV data as file to SFTP via SAP PI SFTP adapter issue
entry.content: Hello! My requirement is send ALV report to SFTP server via SAP PI. I create SFTP receiver and use File Content Conversion. When I set the file format to CSV, Chinese characters are displayed garbled, but when the file format is TXT, it is displayed normally.

entry.title: Quantity Conversion Error for BOMs during MRP run MM-PP
entry.content:
Hi 

We currently have a scenario whereby for material A: 

Alternative Uom are set as follows:

The issue units in Work scheduling is set to ML and WM issue unit is also set to ML.

The Demand created from PIR conversion is correct, the coverts the required 10ML from a BOM to 0,2EA which is correct. however for a demand from a SO, the system does not covert the required 10ML to 0,2EA, instead it round it up to 1EA.

entry.title: Re: Quantity Conversion Error for BOMs during MRP run MM-PP
entry.content: What settings are we missing?

entry.title: Re: In SAP R3 you can compare and analyse differences between MM stock values and FI stock balances
entry.content: @Pieter_vd_B did you find the solution for the mb5l issue?, can you share any alternative solution

entry.title: Re: Extensibility for Manage Prices - Sales
entry.content:
Hello Celine,

Thanks for your time. Let me explain you in detail about the requirement and our approach.

We have to trigger the workflow during the sales order create/change. We created the workflow using Manage Workflow for Sales Configuration and steps and approvers are defined for it.

 

In order to trigger this workflow we implemented the BADI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON 

Here, we need to access the pricing communication data and check if any cline item has the discount(CondType : Z192)  greater than the threshold(CondType: Z190) then we have to set the sales order for the approval.

However, this BADI doesnot have the pricing communication as the importing parameter which we can use to compare the value of the condition types, so we tried to check whether CDS I_SalesDocItemPricingElement returns the runtime value of the pricing for the sales order which is not yet created, which ofcourse didnot return the value as Sales Order is not created yet. This could have been achieved only if the we have pricing communication data available to use in this BADI.

Since it is not, we tried to see if we can create custom field in pricing communication structure and extend it to sales order item, that might flow these amounts from pricing to sales order item field. But in business process i could only see the other way round i.e. flowing from item to pricing communication as shown here in this blog too.

I hope I was able to explain the problem statement here, apologies if anything is not clear and please let me know on that so that I could be more clear :).

Thanks in advance.

Cheers.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD
entry.content: yes, in case you installed the Sample Content "SAP Datasphere Sample Content for FI, HR and SD" already, the best is to delete all objects with the naming convention starts with "SAP_SMPL" and then start with the Data Product and then the Sample Content. Then it should work.

entry.title: Re: Sybase IQ 16.1 OLEDB driver problem with SQL Server I...
entry.content: Hello RolandKramer, Sybase IQ database is running on other server (SAP IQ/16.1.040.2016/17091/P/SP04.14/MS/Windows 2012 R2/64bit). The issue occurs when we want to connect via dboledb.dll in Visual Studio Data Projects that is running on other server with OS Windows Server 2022. We tried 2019 version but we have failed with the same error. We believe that this issue came to light after certain Windows Updates from mid 2023.

entry.title: Re: How to solve simple requirement with RAP, Fiori Eleme...
entry.content:
Dear Patrick

I tried once more to understand your proposed solution. As I wrote earlier I still doubt that your solution is correct and covers my use case which should display a list of employees (from SAP HR Tables PAxxxx) first and only then in the Object Page the CRUD Operations for the Documents of a selected Employee.

I relly can't see how your root view entity ZPW_I_EMPL (which has your basic Table zpw_empl in the "select") could display a list of existing employees from table PAxxxx.

From  your attachment:

define root view entity ZPW_I_EMPL
as select from zpw_empl
composition [0..*] of ZPW_I_DOC as _Doc
{
@ui.facet: [ {
id: 'ZPW_I_DOC',
purpose: #STANDARD,
type: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE,
label: 'Document',
position: 1 ,
targetElement: '_Doc'
} ]
@ui.lineItem: [ {
position: 1
} ]
key employee_id as EmployeeId,
_Doc
}

I would very much appreciate if you could elaborate your proposed solution to me. 
Thank you very much.

Kind regards
Paul

PS:
I guess the ommitance of projection views and Metadata is due to the fact, that it was only "a quick and dirty" approach.

entry.title: SAP CAP - Setting up Multitenancy & using Extensibility for API directory
entry.content:
We are using SAP CAP (node.js) in the Business Application Studio and Cloud Foundry environment and would like to implement some kind of api/middleware. The most important features for our project are multitenancy and extensibility, which should be very similar to the example-project https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/get-started/samples#btp-saas-app - our main focus is on the API folder.

When setting up multitenancy according to the official documentation, the sidecar folder (mtx/sidecar) is auto-generated, but in the example-project this directory is missing / not being used - there is no explanation why.

Which is the correct way to implement multitenancy?

According to the btp-saas-app-sample there is no sidecar-directory used - according to the documentation you should use sidecar-directory.

Is there a way to use Extensibility in API directory?

In ui-directory you can use the extensibility-features, can you also use them in api-directory?

According to the known issues "Extensibility has been temporarily disabled for the CAP API Service".

How to use Hybrid Debug for a specific tenant in multitenancy?

Is there a way to do hybrid debugging using VS Code Debugger and Swagger as a single tenant in the multitenancy scenario? We would like to test specific api-endpoints for different tenants.

 

Thanks in advance.

Given my current financial circumstances, the free attempt at SAP certification would be really helpful as I am in the following category: People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

Thank you for considering my request. I am truly grateful for any support you can provide.

Best regards,
Shaheer.

entry.title: Hello!
entry.content: I'm Sonja from Ukraine, but since 2020 I have been living in Germany and I have experience in Coordination, Project, and Event management. I am happy to be part of this group and so excited to start my learning with SAP!

entry.title: Re: How Do I Copy Master Data Fields to a Sales Document?
entry.content:
Hi @Brinda1,
The custom field in the sales order does not know anything about the customer's freight account information and therefore there is no chance to get the description automatically. It only has the value which you have copied via the Cloud BAdI. If you also want to get the description, you probably need to create a second custom field and write the description into that field.

You could also use a custom field of type "Code List based on CDS View" if there is a released value help available for that field. Unfortunately, I am not an expert in that area. If there is no such value help available, you could request that via customer influence portal.

If you need a fast solution, I would recommend to use a second custom field for the description.

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Extract ADR6 Cust E-Mail Addresses (Business Address Services) Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program
entry.content:
Extract ADR6 Customer E-Mail Addresses (Business Address Services) Data in CSV File Using ABAP Program.

ABAP Program Name ) Z_SZA1_D0100_CUSTOMER         

  •  Program Type – ABAP 
  • ABAP Program Name – Z_SZA1_D0100_CUSTOMER 
  • Tcode - Z_SZA1_CUST  
  • Entity Description - Customer E-Mail Addresses (Business Address Services) Data 
  • Output File Location - “C:\tmp” 
  • Output File Name- SZA1_D0100.CSV  
  • Output File Type – CSV (Column with Semicolon separator (;) 
  • SAP Table Name – ADR6, ADRC, KNA1, KNB1 
  • List of fields to be extracted -  
  • KUNNR 
  • SMTP_ADDR 

---------ABAP PROGRAM START FROM HERE---------------------

REPORT Z_SZA1_D0100_HERO_CUS.

TYPE-POOLS: VRM.

DATA: NAME TYPE VRM_ID,
LIST TYPE VRM_VALUES,
VALUE LIKE LINE OF LIST.


PARAMETERS: ENTITY(10) AS LISTBOX VISIBLE LENGTH 10.
PARAMETERS : OPATH TYPE STRING DEFAULT '\\SERVERNAME\tmp\SZA1_D0100.csv'.

AT SELECTION-SCREEN OUTPUT.

NAME = 'ENTITY'.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK01'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK01'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK75'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK75'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK21'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK21'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK31'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK31'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK41'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK41'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK02'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK02'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK73'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK73'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK04'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK04'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

VALUE-KEY = 'UK61'.
VALUE-TEXT = 'UK61'.
APPEND VALUE TO LIST.

CALL FUNCTION 'VRM_SET_VALUES' EXPORTING ID = NAME VALUES = LIST.

START-OF-SELECTION.

type-pools:TRUXS.
data: begin of itab occurs 0,
KUNNR like KNA1-KUNNR,
SMTP_ADDR like ADR6-SMTP_ADDR,
BUKRS like KNB1-BUKRS,
end of itab.

data: itab1 type TRUXS_T_TEXT_DATA.
SELECT DISTINCT KNA1~KUNNR
ADR6~SMTP_ADDR
KNB1~BUKRS
from KNA1 INNER JOIN ADRC ON KNA1~ADRNR = ADRC~ADDRNUMBER
INNER JOIN ADR6 ON ADRC~ADDRNUMBER = ADR6~ADDRNUMBER
INNER JOIN KNB1 ON KNA1~KUNNR = KNB1~KUNNR
into table itab
WHERE KNB1~BUKRS IN (ENTITY) AND ADR6~FLGDEFAULT IN ('X').

CALL FUNCTION 'SAP_CONVERT_TO_CSV_FORMAT'
EXPORTING
I_FIELD_SEPERATOR = ','
TABLES
I_TAB_SAP_DATA = itab
CHANGING
I_TAB_CONVERTED_DATA = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
CONVERSION_FAILED = 1
OTHERS = 2
.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
MESSAGE ID SY-MSGID TYPE SY-MSGTY NUMBER SY-MSGNO
WITH SY-MSGV1 SY-MSGV2 SY-MSGV3 SY-MSGV4.
ENDIF.

CALL FUNCTION 'GUI_DOWNLOAD'
EXPORTING
filename = OPATH
TABLES
data_tab = itab1
EXCEPTIONS
OTHERS = 1.

entry.title: CDS for Picking Process in Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
Hello, I successfully created an Outbound Delivery using I_OutboundDeliveryTP and EML with ABAP RAP. However, to Post GI for this delivery I couldn't find a way to update picking quantity for items in my outbound delivery. 

How can I do that in ABAP Cloud Private Edition? I attempted to modify the field PickQuantityInOrderUnit of the entity OutboundDeliveryItem, but I get an error that this field is read-only. Moreover, trying to create items of I_OutboundDeliveryTP using _Item association with PickQuantityInOrderUnit set resulted in the same error.

entry.title: Re: Using KeePass Instead of SAP Logon
entry.content:
Did you installed the 64bit version? If so, the installation path is changed.

On the 32bit, the SAP GUI binaries were installed on "c:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\FrontEnd\SAPgui".

entry.title: Re: class proxy->save_document_to_url does not work
entry.content: Create the file in the SAP GUI temporary folder of the user (CL_GUI_FRONTEND_SERVICES=>GET_TEMP_DIRECTORY) and then upload it.

entry.title: Re: SAP BY Design - UK BACS Domestic Payment Format
entry.content:
Hello @HarshalVakil 

We have trouble creating the XSLT tranformation on our own: Do you know if SAP provide any documentation about rules and how to create the XSLT transformation from scratch ?

I found this Generation of Custom Payment File Using XSL Transformation Repository | SAP Help Portal
but no details about the structure and how to create the XSLT file. 

Thank you very much.

Best regards.

entry.title: Have SAP HANA Database Explorer Extension for VSCode in Open VSX
entry.content:
Hello,
I would like to ask if it's possible to publish this extension to the Open VSX marketplace.

## Context

In the company I am working for, we use SAP HANA Database and the new IDE called 'Positron', from the same creators from RStudio. It's based on VSCode, but only accepts extensions published on Open VSX.

Unfortunately, as Microsoft prohibits usages of the Microsoft marketplace by any other products or redistribution of `.vsix` files from it, in order to use VS Code extensions in non-Microsoft products, it would be nice to have the extension published on VS Code extension namespace in [Open VSX](https://open-vsx.org/).

## What is Open VSX? Why does it exist?

Open VSX is a vendor neutral alternative to the MS marketplace used by most other derivatives of VS Code like [VSCodium](https://vscodium.com/), [Gitpod](https://www.gitpod.io), [OpenVSCode](https://github.com/gitpod-io/openvscode-server), [Theia](https://theia-ide.org/)-based IDEs, and so on.

You can read on about Open VSX at the Eclipse Foundation's [Open VSX FAQ](https://www.eclipse.org/legal/open-vsx-registry-faq/).

## How can you publish to Open VSX?

The docs to publish an extension can be found [here](https://github.com/eclipse/openvsx/wiki/Publishing-Extensions). This process is straightforward and shouldn't take too long. Essentially, you need an authentication token and to execute the `ovsx publish` command to publish your extension. There's also [a doc](https://github.com/open-vsx/publish-extensions/blob/master/docs/direct_publish_setup.md) explaining the whole process with an example GitHub Action workflow.

 

Thanks you so much.

SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database 

@thomas_jung @dan_van

entry.title: Re: Where do I get the Course Files (zip file) for practi...

Hello @yamile81,

The Course Files is embedded in the lesson. Unit 1 lesson 1. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Move / Copy file from one folder to another folder
entry.content:
CALL FUNCTION 'FTP_COMMAND'
EXPORTING
handle = lv_ftp_handle
command = 'RNFR D:\Sales\Macine\Product\fail_log\test.csv'
IMPORTING
reply = lv_reply.CALL FUNCTION 'FTP_COMMAND'
EXPORTING
handle = lv_ftp_handle
command = 'RNTO D:\Sales\Macine\Product\fail_log_n\test.csv'
IMPORTING
reply = lv_reply.                                                                                                                                          

I also have a similar requirement. the thing is this is rename right. my lead says we dont have the authorization to rename. we need to transfer files among two folders within same ftp, server  read and copy to another program. Is there any way.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
How is the Tier-3 model upgrade-safe? 

Even though the unreleased APIs are in Tier-3, those objects are invoked during the execution of the objects in Tier-1 via Tier-2. Imagine a situation where SAP modifies an unreleased object, which is obviously not guaranteed by SAP. So, how do the objects in Tier-1 maintain "stable functionality"?

entry.title: Effectivity Parameter definition is incorrect
entry.content:
I am Trying PEO. But while creating the effectivity parameter getting the below issue.

SIMG - Production - Manufacturing for Production Engineering and Operations - Cross-Applications - Unitized Product - Configure Parameters for Product Unitization

In Effectivity types I am using the below.

SIMG - Cross-Application Components - Engineering Change Management - Parameter Effectivity - Define Effectivity Types

What is the error in configs?

entry.title: Re: 🟢 What Software Developers Should Know About Key User Extensibility
entry.content:
Hi Thomas,

great session thanks - very interesting to see the latest features in this area.

Regarding UI Adaptation Mode. I've been working with S/4HANA cloud, private edition 2020 release, in which some apps support UI Adaptation Mode, but many don't, or at least not to any useful extent. I'm not clear if it's only the Fiori Elements based apps that support it, but not freestyle SAPUI5 apps? Or is it that either UI technology can support it, but the app developer must have taken some steps to enable it?

In the most recent 2023 release, should we expect that business apps generally will now support UI Adaptation Mode? Or is it mostly only apps that are also available in S/4HANA cloud, public edition?

thanks, Roger

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Scripting - Part 1
entry.content:
Hello,

thanks for your effort, but...

Doing this configuration and cleanup work manually in dialog mode - even if it is done with GUI scripting - is really completely outdated. It's error-prone, slow, causes a lot of effort and I would say this approach leads to incompleteness in the result. All disadvantages you mention in chapter 1 of your article also applies to this approach.

There are various tools for this, both from SAP (PCA) and from third-party vendors. All of them (that I know of) go the route of exporting the system specific data before the system refresh and then cleaning/importing with R3trans. Just google “saving system specific data with R3trans”.

Kind regards Jan

entry.title: Re: SAP Workzone: handling Knowledge Base and Documents e...
entry.content:
Dear Leila,

there is currently no such feature within SAP Build Work Zone advanced edition for expiring documents or KB articles available.

You always have the option to create a feature request in influence.sap.com, in case you don't want to wait, you might have a look in the capabilities with the webhooks and add your own custom logic/development.

Kind regards, Jan

entry.title: Re: MB52 Layout - Missing field/option
entry.content: MB52_NOVAL missing

entry.title: Re: BNKA bank codes for Spain
entry.content:
Hey, we are using the SAP specific BDE files in the meantime which are also provided by Swift. And these files contain all the 8-digit bank keys.

Best, Robert

I'm looking for the exact expo or other command that is used to bild the application.

entry.title: Re: New 2H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content: What a great blog post with great insights on all the exciting new features! My favorites are especially all the UI changes and enhancements. Thank you, Volker! Your insights sure will be missed! All the best for your next chapter!

entry.title: Re: SAP for Energy & Utilities "Espresso Sessions"
entry.content:
The next exciting Espresso Session for Oil, Gas, and Energy, "Optimizing Fuel Retail Operations" will take place on 6th November 2024
Registration page

entry.title: Re: Restrict logon through RFC remote logon
entry.content:
Hello,

I would remove debugging (with edit) from your firefighter user.

If you want to habe it still, why do you not locl the user?

Our firefighter users are always locked. If you want to use them, you have to write an incident ticket to SAP Basis to unlock them. There is also a list of people, who are allowed to do so.

My 2 cents.

entry.title: Re: BNKA bank codes for Spain
entry.content: Hello Robert, thanks for your reply. Where could we get this file with 8 digit bank keys from? Many thanks

entry.title: Re: Developing with SAP Integration Suite - Wrong exercise in the lesson.
entry.content: Hi @Laxcen, Thanks for pointing this out. The issue has been fixed. Best, Kerstin

entry.title: SAP B1 rsp services stop after restart server
entry.content:
Dear Support Team,

I would like to inform you that whenever the SAP application server is restarted, the SAP RSP services stop and do not restart automatically. The services remain down until they are manually started.

Kindly look into this issue and advise on a solution.

 

Regards

Neeraj Upadhyay

entry.title: Call Transaction CJ20n and jump on specific Milestone
entry.content:
Hi guys,

I would like to call the Transaction CJ20n (via ABAP), skip first screen and jump directly on a specific Milestone. Any ideas how to develop that?

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: BNKA bank codes for Spain
entry.content: Hey, you need a corresponding contract with Swift and in your account you will then find a download area for the files (see https://www.swiftrefdata.com/new/en/download). With the new contract we have switched from the IBAN PLUS file to the Bank Directory SAP file. Best, Robert

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Really looking forward to the extended ATC for Tier-3 developments. Big news for everyone who has to deal with a large amount of classic-ABAP Dynpro developments.

entry.title: Re: EWM - Vendor Returns Process - Returns Delivery Vs Returns PO
entry.content:
Dang,
Thank you for sharing , but after the test , still have a question.

if i don't mark Vendor as Return Supplier，then generated outbound delivery's receiving party is the purchase's factory ；if i mark Vendor as Return Supplier，then generated outbound delivery's receiving party is the customer number i'd maintained，so is this logic right?

Looking forward to your reply.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @Ramjee_korada ,

for Tier 2 developments you should use the Classic APIs for tier 2. They are unreleased for ABAP Cloud but stable and will not change. 

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: Copy Partner from STO PO to Outbound Delivery
entry.content:
Hi @ISoma,

We need to go for BADI or Enhancement in your case for transferring the partner functions from STO to Delivery.

The system does not transfer the partner data from the STO to the replenishment delivery.

kindly check the point no 2 in SAP note - 842829 - Characteristics of replenishment deliveries from STOs for more info.

Best Regards,
Surya.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 
Hope You are doing well,
I have completing the SAP Learning Journey Developing Applications on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime... Below attached Screen shot. Please tell me further procedure.

entry.title: RAP - Use of /SAPSLL/CL_PRCLS_API
entry.content:
Hi

I'm trying to use /SAPSLL/CL_PRCLS_API to classify/re-classify products as per note 2458080. 

The class is using BOBF framework, and the save methods are using commits, which is illegal. Therefore I have implemented the cl_abap_behavior_saver to do the save logic. But no luck.
I could create a RFC and call using "Destination "NONE"". But absolutely not the recommended way.

What could I do, to keep the code up-to-date?

entry.title: How to Update DMEE Format CGI_XML_CT to the pain 001.001.09 Version?
entry.content:
Hello I am using an ECC System with version APPL 618. 

I Want to update the current version of my DMEE Format CGI_CML_CT to the latest version, as it is still the version pain 001.001.03. 

As described in the note: 2253571 I implemented all notes and did the manual activity described for my solution. 

I am stuck when it comes to downloading the new dmee format from the attachments. 

The note refers to a file in step 4 of the manual activities: 

  • To update the version of the DME Tree format, please download the attached file CGI_XML_CT_BAdI_MMDDYY.XML to your local drive and continue with the steps described below.

Sadly this File does not exist in the attachements! There is no file with "BAdI" in the filename. 

Also there is the CGI_XML_CT_V9 Format, which should in theory contain the 9th version of the format, as it is called "V9". 

Sadly, when I download the file, it says that the format is still the pain 001.001.03 Version. 

I just feel that the note refers to and contains just old formats. 

Where can I download the DMEE Instruction file for the CGI version 001.001.09? 

Thanks in advance!

#dmee #cgi #paymentmedium #ecc

entry.title: Re: How to change message "One selected object canno...
entry.content: Hi. I have it but no changes.

entry.title: Re: How to change message "One selected object canno...
entry.content: Identification: [ { $Type : 'UI.DataFieldForAction', Action: 'CatalogService.Submit4Approval', Label : '{i18n>Submit4Approval}' }, { $Type : 'UI.DataFieldForAction', Action: 'CatalogService.Approve', Label : '{i18n>Approve}' }, { $Type : 'UI.DataFieldForAction', Action: 'CatalogService.Reject', Label : '{i18n>Reject}}' }, { $Type: 'UI.DataField', Value: wbs } ],

Re: React Native app deployment to SAP BTP
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja ,

Ramjee Korada

entry.title: Charm User unable Change status
entry.content:
Hello Team,

 

User unable Change status form successfully test to Authorized to Pre-PRD Unable set previous status

entry.title: Re: React Native app deployment to SAP BTP
entry.content:
Hi,

1. The command we are using to build the app is `npx expo export --platform web`.

2. After this the command `mbt build` is run to generate the makefile.

3. Then to deploy, the command `cf deploy mta_archives/<your-app-mtar-archive-name>.mtar` is run.

Also, we are not getting the same Deprecation Warning when running or building the app

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

Hope you are doing well.

Please Share Free Certification code with me.

Thank You.

entry.title: Re: use case for "Application.getfiledatasource" ?
entry.content:
Hi @oliver_raths,

I know your question ist quite old and you surely have found a solution. But since I just stumbled upon this discussion and for everyone else who may find this discussion, I´d like to add the following:

You must use the ID of the model, not the technical name or description. The ID will look like "Cu4o1l0eqjiphse1tosogajc2u".

You can look up the ID in the model "RESOURCE", which is available on every SAC. Please filter the "Object Name" to the technical name of your model (like your "testquery") and output the "Object ID". This is the ID you want to use in Application.getfiledatasource.

So the coding in your example will look like:

ds = getFiledatasource("Cu4o1l0eqjiphse1tosogajc2u");
ds.setVariableValue("0I_FPER",{from:time_range_from_array[1],to:time_range_to_array[1]});

Best regards,
Denis

entry.title: Where can I start learning to configure and integrate Joule, SAP’s AI copilot?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Provisioning and Implementing Joule learning journey

Overview
This learning journey offers a comprehensive approach to understanding, configuring, and integrating Joule, SAP’s AI copilot, across various SAP platforms, including SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. It will guide you through both the functional aspects of leveraging Joule to boost productivity for your customers and the technical configurations needed to set it up effectively.

Learning objectives

After completing this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Master fundamental functionalities of Joule in the SAP landscape
  • Set up Joule with SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Understand how to commercialize Joule for customers
  • Troubleshoot common setup issues
  • Customize Joule to meet specific business needs
  • Stay updated with the developments in Joule through ongoing learning and community engagement

Prerequisites

  • Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio

 Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: CCO with SAP B1 MENA localization
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Does CCO supports MENA localization in SAP business one? if not, will it be on the short term? 

What will be the limitations when using CCO with MENA localization? is it something that can be handled using CCO plugins?

 

Regards.

Ramzi Al-Khouri.

entry.title: Re: ERROR: Upgrading @cap/cds and @cap/cds-dk to version 8
entry.content: Hi! Could you please share a version where you are using the @sap/cds versions of 7.9.3? I tried to run a version in dev mode where I downgraded the @sap/cds libraries to 7.9.3, but I get the following compile error: Compilation error in src/odata/odata.module.ts [ERROR] 12:39:16 ⨯ Unable to compile TypeScript: src/odata/odata.module.ts(19,11): error TS2345: Argument of type 'AbstractHttpAdapter<any, any, any>' is not assignable to parameter of type 'Application'.

entry.title: Re: Case Sensitivity Issue with ListPicker in SAP MDK 24.4
entry.content: @carlos_sanchez8 could you share what do you have in your rule? and the definition for the list picker control?

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Transforming legacy code to CAP with the help of GenAI
entry.content:
Thank you for the great session.

My question about  what GenAI Model is used was answered with: GPT-4o

But here is another one: How many Input / Output Tokens where needed for SHINE? (To estimate the costs).

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

My name is Rina and I am from Vietnam.

I just switched from a procurement job to a freelance SAP consulting job earlier this year, therefore my knowledge regarding SAP is still limited. Because I'm not yet certified for any SAP certification, my goal is to get certified and I have been self-studying to achieve this goal.

Hearing about the digital skills initiative application, I think this is a great opportunity for me as a Vietnamese woman to access more knowledge regarding SAP. I also think this is a great opportunity for me to help build the SAP community in Vietnam since SAP in Vietnam is still relatively new.

Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon.

Best regards,

Rina

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @Ramjee_korada ,

if using Classic API (which is stable but not released for ABAP Cloud) in tier 2 you will get ATC info message (prio 3) during cloud readiness check.

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: ERROR: Upgrading @cap/cds and @cap/cds-dk to version 8
entry.content:
Hi sorry!
Yes, I have now pushed a version where the error you described no longer occurs.

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

I identify as these categories below:

  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts (live in one of the poorest counties in CA)
  • Minorities who may not have equal opportunity in their geographic location 
  • First generation college students
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life
  • People who lack the financial means to afford a certification attempt for themselves

I am willing to learn about SAP and hopefully pass certification. My first learning journey that I am working on is Configuring SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration.  I am hoping to learn more learning journey's on SAP implementation and finance too.  Currently, I work in the laboratory, but hoping to use this opportunity to work under business consulting and get into the supply chain field.

entry.title: Re: Move / Copy file from one folder to another folder
entry.content: Please post a new question here and see also the List of FTP commands - Wikipedia (mainly RFC 959).

entry.title: Re: ERROR: Upgrading @cap/cds and @cap/cds-dk to version 8
entry.content: Hi sorry! Yes, I have now pushed a version where the error you described no longer occurs.

entry.title: Use of BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CHANGE to change a value in RBKP table
entry.content:
I'm trying to lift the payment block using RM08RELEASE. While it was successful, the payment block remains in the RBKP for the invoices. 

Tried to use BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CHANGE and BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT but the payment block in RBKP was not deleted or changed.

What more should I check?

 

DATA: LS_HEADER01 TYPE BAPI_INCINV_CHNG_HEADER,
      LS_HEADER02 TYPE BAPI_INCINV_CHNG_HEADER.

LS_HEADER01-INV_DOC_NO = '12345'
LS_HEADER01-INV_YEAR = '2024'
LS_HEADER02-PMNT_BLOCK = ' '.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CHANGE'
 EXPORTING 
  INVOICENUMBER = LS_HEADER01-INV_DOC_NO
  FISCALYEAR    = LS_HEADER01-INV_YEAR
  HEADERDATA_CHANGE = LS_HEADER01
  HEADERDATA_CHANGEX = LS_HEADER02
 TABLES
  RETURN        = IT_RETURN
IF sy-subrc = 0.
 CALL FUNCTION BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT
  IMPORTING
    RETURN = IT_RETURN
ENDIF

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @OlgaDolinskaja 

Thanks for quick feedback. Logically the same ATC message would be raised even in Tier 3. What is the value add in creating wrapper other than segregating? My perspective is that Tier-2 would be a redundant and overhead for classic released ( not cloud ready ) APIs. 

Best wishes, Ramjee Korada

entry.title: Re: Call Transaction CJ20n and jump on specific Milestone
entry.content: I guess you tried Batch Input on CJ20N and it doesn't work because it's an Enjoy transaction (SAP note 535013 - CJ20N: batch input in background mode https://me.sap.com/notes/535013/E). Did you try CJ20?

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content: My name is Abdelrahman, I'm a highly motivated individual with a strong interest in technology and a passion for learning new things. I'm excited to embark on a career in SAP and contribute my skills to innovative projects.

entry.title: S/4 HANA trial on GCP with 90-day, $300 Free Trial offer
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

is it possible to use the S/4 HANA 30-day free trial in combination with the 90-day, $300 Free trial offer on GCP? While setting up the system, I received the following error: 

 
Usage limits reached: Regional: - Quota 'SSD_TOTAL_GB' exceeded. Available: 500, Requested: 964 To solve this problem you have to request any additional quota from the Quotas page in the GCP console.
More Information
GCP Documentation
 
Additional Quota on the other hand cannot be acquired with the 90-day, $300 Free Trial offer:
 

 

 
Has anyone found a way to use both trials (S/4 HANA and GCP) combined and if so how?

entry.title: Re: Error in intgration CPI-Successfactors using OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion
entry.content: Hi, In Step3, you should not generate the X509 certificate in Successfactors. Instead you need to copy and paste the certificate which was created in Step2. In the screen shots i see Step2 and Step3 certificates are different.

entry.title: Re: Using the Destination service in SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry Environment
entry.content:
Hello mariusobert,

Thanks for the amazing blog.

I know you will feel Deja Vu when you read this comment cause every other comment is regarding the 404 issue while calling the API from Fiori app.

I have tried a lot of things but nothing seems to work, so here i am asking the experts.

Context : I have created a CAP project "manish" on BAS, in this project i've added a UI app "manishtest".

now i want to call "Northwind" destination from "manishtest" application

some dummy data and code has been added so that the application can run after deployment.(im only testing this after deployment in HTML5 Repo of my subaccount).

here is the code for :

1. xs-app.json 

 

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/odata/(.*)$",
      "target": "/odata/$1",
      "destination": "manish-srv-api",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },
    {
      "source": "^/northwind/(.*)$",
      "target": "/northwind/$1",
      "destination": "Northwind",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

 

2. mta file of the CAP project

 

_schema-version: 3.3.0
ID: manish
description: A simple CAP project.
version: 1.0.0
modules:
  - name: manish-srv
    type: nodejs
    path: gen/srv
    requires:
      - name: uaa_manish
      - name: manish-db
    provides:
      - name: srv-api
        properties:
          srv-url: ${default-url}
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
      readiness-health-check-http-endpoint: /health
      readiness-health-check-type: http
    build-parameters:
      builder: npm
  - name: manish-destination-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    requires:
      - name: manish-destination-service
        parameters:
          content-target: true
      - name: manish_html_repo_host
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: manish_html_repo_host-key
      - name: uaa_manish
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: uaa_manish-key
    parameters:
      content:
        instance:
          destinations:
            - Name: manish_router_manish_html_repo_host
              ServiceInstanceName: manish-html5-app-host-service
              ServiceKeyName: manish_html_repo_host-key
              sap.cloud.service: manish-router
            - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              Name: manish_router_uaa_manish
              ServiceInstanceName: manish-xsuaa-service
              ServiceKeyName: uaa_manish-key
              sap.cloud.service: manish-router
          existing_destinations_policy: ignore
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true
  - name: manish-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: manish_html_repo_host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - manishtest.zip
          name: manishtest
          target-path: resources/
  - name: manishtest
    type: html5
    path: app/manishtest
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms:
        []

  - name: manish-db-deployer
    type: hdb
    path: gen/db
    parameters:
      buildpack: nodejs_buildpack
    requires:
      - name: manish-db
resources:
  - name: manish-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                HTML5.DynamicDestination: true
                HTML5.ForwardAuthToken: true
                Name: manish-srv-api
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: ~{srv-api/srv-url}
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: manish-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
    requires:
      - name: srv-api
  - name: manish_html_repo_host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: manish-html5-app-host-service
      service-plan: app-host
  - name: uaa_manish
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-name: manish-xsuaa-service
      service-plan: application
      config:
        xsappname: manish
        tenant-mode: dedicated
  - name: manish-db
    type: com.sap.xs.hdi-container
    parameters:
      service: hana
      service-plan: hdi-shared
parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true
build-parameters:
  before-all:
    - builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm ci
        - npx cds build --production

 

3. package.json of the project 

 

{
  "name": "manish",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "A simple CAP project.",
  "repository": "<Add your repository here>",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "@sap/cds": "^8",
    "express": "^4",
    "@cap-js/hana": "^1",
    "@sap/xssec": "^4"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/cds-types": "^0.6",
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^1",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^8",
    "rimraf": "^5.0.5",
    "@sap/ux-specification": "UI5-1.129"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cds-serve",
    "watch-manishtest": "cds watch --open manishtest/webapp/index.html?sap-ui-xx-viewCache=false",
    "undeploy": "cf undeploy manish --delete-services --delete-service-keys --delete-service-brokers",
    "build": "rimraf resources mta_archives && mbt build --mtar archive",
    "deploy": "cf deploy mta_archives/archive.mtar --retries 1"
  },
  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "auth": "xsuaa"
    }
  }
}

 

4. xs-security.json of the project

 

{
  "xsappname": "manish",
  "tenant-mode": "dedicated",
  "description": "Security profile of called application",
  "scopes": [
    {
      "name": "uaa.user",
      "description": "UAA"
    }
  ],
  "role-templates": [
    {
      "name": "Token_Exchange",
      "description": "UAA",
      "scope-references": [
        "uaa.user"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "attributes": []
}

 

everything works fine except for the Northwind API issue.

Here is the Destination config

this destination is currently in service instance, but i have tried with the subaccount level destination also.

here is the code from the controller where im trying to get the northwind data.

 

sap.ui.define([
    "sap/ui/core/mvc/Controller"
],
function (Controller) {
    "use strict";

    return Controller.extend("manishtest.controller.worklist", {
        onInit: function () {

        },
        // function to fetch northwind data
        fetchNorthwindData : function(){

            var appId = this.getOwnerComponent().getManifestEntry("/sap.app/id");

            var appPath = appId.replaceAll(".", "/");

            var appModulePath = jQuery.sap.getModulePath(appPath);

            $.ajax({
                type: "GET",
                url: appModulePath + '/northwind/V4/Northwind/Northwind.svc/Products',
                success: function ({ value }) {
                  console.log(value);
                }
            });
          }
    });
});

 

and now finally here is the  WEIRED responses im getting,

first is the request with status 302 redirect, seems like this is valid Behavior to me but idk.

the url also looks fine, issue is in the 2nd api call with the 404 response status.

i dont know how and where that 

https://development-oc58dg9d.launchpad.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/(S(xliwi4o5yctd1xglvqu3m2sk))/northwind/V4/Northwind/Northwind.svc/Products

text in red is coming from, also the response is 404 so im not able to consume the data from that destination.

please help, ive been trying to do this for a week now but im not getting anywhere.

any kind of help is appreciated.

Thanks, Manish.

ill just tag some big names lol @thomas_jung , @qmacro

entry.title: Re: Use of BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CHANGE to change a value in RBKP table
entry.content: As far as I have in mind the payment block for MM invoices is linked with the blocking reasons price, quantity, quality,... stored in the position table RSEG. Have you checked your position table before?

entry.title: FM ATP_BOP_API_REQUIREMENTS_RUN
entry.content:
Can anyone please tell  what is the purpose of the FM ATP_BOP_API_REQUIREMENTS_RUN? Also, can anyone please provide code for this FM.

@SAPSupport @DominikTylczyn @Mrak1 @fabio_perfetto @Margit_Wagner @AndreasMuno @Dsk @Enda @Christian_Ries @tamas_hoznek

entry.title: Start this week the Online Lectures in SAP Inside Track Fortaleza 2024
entry.content:
Start this week the Online Lectures in SAP Inside Track Fortaleza

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nSwrvl182aY

Here is the link to the Online Lectures for SAP Inside Track Fortaleza.

https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1X2eHF6nga_5E6nTWRKR9RHn6pGMXGrs5vbYsFJDmerk/edit?usp=sharing

The lectures will be broadcast in YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC_eLPzj_iyIvNzrhggx_rSg

entry.title: Re: Error while accessing KBA
entry.content: Hi @Sandra_Rossui, I was able to access the KBA using a different special access account provided by company.

entry.title: Transitioning to SapMachine: Preparing for the Future of SAP JVM in SAP Focused Run
entry.content:
In today’s rapidly evolving technological landscape, staying current with the latest software updates is essential to ensure security, performance, and reliability. With this in mind, SAP is excited to announce its shift from SAP JVM 8 to SapMachine—SAP’s very own production-grade, open-source version of the Java Development Kit (JDK). This transition marks a significant milestone in SAP’s commitment to providing a robust Java environment that aligns with modern security and performance standards.

What is SapMachine?

SapMachine is a free, open-source implementation of the OpenJDK, built and maintained by SAP. It offers a fully functional, cross-platform, and production-ready JDK, certified to meet Java SE standards. Following the established OpenJDK release cycle, SapMachine ships a new feature release every six months and provides quarterly updates for active versions. Long-term support (LTS) releases are delivered every two years, ensuring that SAP systems continue to benefit from regular performance improvements and timely security updates.

For SAP and its customers, SapMachine is more than just a JDK. It serves as the engine behind countless cloud and on-premise applications, offering a reliable foundation to run everything from small desktop programs to large-scale server applications.

Why the Move from SAP JVM 8 to SapMachine?

Focusing on SAP Focused Run product, starting with SAP Focused Run 4.0 FP03, SAP will enable the Simple Diagnostics Agent (SDA) to run on either SapMachine 21 or SAPJVM 8. However, with SAP Focused Run 5.0, SapMachine will become mandatory, and SAPJVM 8 will no longer be supported. This transition is part of SAP’s strategy to ensure ongoing security, performance, and future-proofing of Java-based environments.

SapMachine 21, which will be delivered via the SAP Software Download Center, brings with it several key benefits:

  • Up-to-date security patches: By using the latest JDK version, SAP will be able to deliver security fixes faster and with greater safety.
  • Open-source flexibility: Customers will have access to an open-source solution that adheres to Java SE standards.
  • Long-term support: SapMachine 21 will serve as an LTS release, ensuring stability and regular updates for years to come.

Timeline for Migration

To ensure a smooth transition to SapMachine, SAP has laid out the following roadmap:

  • November 2024: SAP Focused Run 5.0 will be available, and customers are encouraged to begin migrating to SapMachine.
  • November 2025: End-of-maintenance for SAP Focused Run 4.0. From this point on, SapMachine will be mandatory for all platforms previously supported by SAPJVM 8.

Customers are encouraged to begin planning their migration now to avoid any disruptions as SAP phases out support for SAPJVM 8.

Preparing for Migration: What You Need to Know

  • Supported Platforms: SapMachine is available for a variety of platforms, including AIX, Linux Little Endian, Linux x86, and Windows. However, it is important to note that HP-UX, Solaris x64, and Solaris SPARC will not be supported by SapMachine.
  • Deployment: The deployment process for SapMachine remains unchanged from SAPJVM. You will simply need to upload the appropriate SapMachine archive from the SAP Software Download Center for your platform, and SAP Focused Run will deploy it accordingly.

Moving Forward: Start Planning Your Migration Today

As we move forward, we highly encourage all SAP customers to begin planning their migration to SapMachine as soon as possible. While the shift from SAPJVM 8 to SapMachine represents a significant change, it also offers a great opportunity to modernize and secure your Java environments for the long term.

Our dedicated support team is ready to assist you through every step of this transition. If you have any questions or need guidance during the migration process, please don’t hesitate to reach out.

Prepare today, and ensure your systems are ready for the future with SapMachine—SAP’s trusted, open-source JDK.

Downloading SAP MACHINE software

From SAP For Me portal, navigate through Services and Support / Download Software to access Software Center.

Run search by SAP MACHINE, then select SAP MACHINE 21 as software category:

Finally, select Operating System version first, then download latest available version released:

The update JVM procedure is not changing with this new JDK, you can review it from SAP Focused Run Masterguide under SAP Help Portal, section Preparing Simple Diagnostics Agent Distribution.

“Upload the binaries to SAP Focused Run by running the report SRSM_AMA_UPLOAD_BINARY with transaction

SA38. Refer to the security guide section on report SRSM_AMA_UPLOAD_BINARY and ensure that virus

scanning is activated”

Once the update is done, you can check new version released under table AMA_SETUP_INFO

Related links:

  • SAP Machine on GitHub, full documentation 
  • 3426855 - Simple Diagnostics Agent running on next generation Java VM
  • SAP Help Portal for SAP Focused Run
  • 2700275 - Java SE Technologies in SAP Products
  • SAPMACHINE podcast talking about new OpenJDK release

entry.title: Re: Error while accessing KBA
entry.content: Hi @Sandra_Rossi, I was able to access the KBA using a different special access account provided by company. Thank you for your help.

entry.title: Re: Deep linking to SAP SuccessFactors Standard Portlets and Custom MDF portlets
entry.content: Does anyone know how you can make this work for the new People Profile as it doesn't seem supported?

entry.title: Re: Review Booklet Designer (Concept and Adaptability) - CE2408
entry.content: When I open the app Manage KPIS and Reports, the button 'Review Booklet', next to 'Stories' is missing. We are using CE2408, and the roles in place. Any guesses?

entry.title: Re: Call Transaction CJ20n and jump on specific Milestone
entry.content:
Hi @Sandra_Rossi 

thank you for your reply. No, I didn't try it over BatchInput. I wanted to do it via the ABAP Statement 'Call Transaction CJ20n'. But there I can only give WBS Elements as Input but not Milestones etc.

entry.title: UI5 freestyle development in SAP Build Code with Joule
entry.content:
SAP Build code, which focuses on pro-code development, offers a variety of GenAI functionalties to support the development process using CAP, HANA development, as well as UI5.

In this blog post, I'll focus on the freestyle aspect of UI5 development, demonstrating how to get started with a freestyle UI5 application. My aim is to showcase how Joule can be used to both accelerate the development and provide support for those who may not be UI5 experts to the very fingertips.

The following simple scenario will give you a glimpse into developing applications with SAP Build Code. Using the BTP Trial version you can currently setup a SAP Build Code instance to try Joule’s GenAI capabilities.

 

Getting Started

  1. Open the SAP Build Lobby from the BTP Subaccount Cockpit under Services > Instances and Subscriptions > SAP Build Code.
  2. Create a new SAP Build Code Fiori, Full-Stack, or Mobile application. For this scenario, a SAP Fiori Application is suitable.
  3. Enter a project name. If no dev space exists, it will be prefilled to create a new Fiori dev space:

4. Once the project is created and the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) dev space starts, click on the project name to open BAS.

 

Creating a UI5 Application

Create a UI5 application using the template wizard in BAS:

  1. Go to Get Started > New Project from Template > SAP Fiori Generator
  2. Use the Basic template to start from scratch
  3. For the OData service, create a destination to the OData V2 as an example OData connection
  4.  Add a view name – In my case Main
  5. Optionally, set the deployment configuration and add the app to the managed application router if you want to deploy and consume the app from within SAP Build Work Zone

 

Leveraging Joule for UI5 Development

Recently, major UX updates were introduced to the Joule capabilities, moving from mainly being able to access Joule from the Guide Center towards a single chat, where users can use different slash commands to invoke tailored Joule functionalities.

For UI5 questions and development the /ui5 slash command is there to achieve this:

 

Example: Creating a Product Table

In the yet empty project, in order to see the /ui5 slash command, we firstly need to open the controller or view that was created from the template. Afterwards, the /ui5 slash command is suggested and we can directly start with a sample prompt to show products of the OData service:

"/ui5 create a table in the main view that displays the products of the northwind service. Provide data bindings to the properties of this odata entity."

After invoking the prompt, Joule already looked up files considered important for executing the task:

In the answer, Joule provided the code in the Main.xml.view  including the product table. Since the screenshot of the whole response is large and thus difficult to paste here, this is the code it provided :

 

<mvc:View controllerName="demoapp.controller.Main"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" displayBlock="true"
    xmlns="sap.m">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <content>
            <Table id="productTable" items="{
                path: '/Products'
            }">
                <headerToolbar>
                    <Toolbar>
                        <Title text="Products" />
                    </Toolbar>
                </headerToolbar>
                <columns>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Product ID" />
                    </Column>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Product Name" />
                    </Column>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Quantity Per Unit" />
                    </Column>
                    <Column>
                        <Text text="Unit Price" />
                    </Column>
                </columns>
                <items>
                    <ColumnListItem>
                        <cells>
                            <Text text="{ProductID}" />
                            <Text text="{ProductName}" />
                            <Text text="{QuantityPerUnit}" />
                            <Text text="{UnitPrice}" />
                        </cells>
                    </ColumnListItem>
                </items>
            </Table>
        </content>
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

As you can see, the suggestions looked good, also containing data binding to 4 properties of the Products entity. That already saves some manually coding time for developers and can be further adapted to include more columns and properties.

An explanation at the end is also provided:

Afterwards, the application can be previewed using the green play button in BAS for example. The result already looks promising:

 

Formatting and Adding Functionality

Now, I wanted to format the Unit Price to make it look a little bit nicer and also display  the sign of the Euro currency after the number. Additionally, I wanted to create a custom column with some sort of action that can be invoked on each product. So, Joule was asked again:

"/ui5 format the unit price in the table to round to 2 decimals and include an euro sign after the number. Create a formatter file with a function for this. Additionally, include another column in the table named Order where each cell is composed of a button that indicated that the user wants to buy this product. Provide a basic press event handler one of this button is pressed."

Multiple files were suggested to be adapted and/or changed, such as creating a formatter.js file to format the UnitPrice property.

On top of that, it created a column Order, a function onPressOrder in the Main.controller.js file, and a press event in the Main.view.xml in order to show an UI5 MessageToast of the order placed for a certain product:

 

To further add some application logic, Joule assisted to create a condition when clicking the button:

“/ui5 please change the onOrderPress function that checks if the unit price of the product is above 50 €. If this is the case, show a message dialog indicating that the products above 50 € can currently not be ordered.“

Result, only when clicking a product over 50 (€):

 

Using AI-Powered Code Completion

Besides Joule, since very recently SAP Build Code introduced code completion and suggestions directly in the editor. Therefore, we can also make use of this AI feature.In order to show this, I added a comment in the onOrderPress code and described that I want to have a confirmation dialog instead of a message toast. Shortly after, a code suggestion was directly available in the function:

I had to remove the MessageToast line of code, but besides that, it immediately worked:

 

Understanding Your Code with Joule

Coming to the end, one more thing to highlight: Using Joule to help explaining code. As such, the following prompt was sent: 

“/ui5 what does the main view do?”

Here is an extract of Joule’s answer, which helps to understand the code even further:

 

Conclusion

As you can see, SAP Build Code with Joule supports a variety of use cases for UI5 development, in addition to its other GenAI focus on CAP and Full-Stack development. Please keep in mind that prompt responses can vary, thus potentially leading to slighty different code proposals. Nonetheless, it's a powerful feature that can enhance your development process, whether you're an experienced UI5 developer or just getting started. Let’s see what the future brings with more and more features getting introduced!

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Brisbane #1 - 14th November 2024
entry.content: Looking forward to it.  I'm at Oktoberfest at the German club this weekend 🍻 . So will have to bring some of the Oktoberfest spirit along with me.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Developing and Transforming your ABAP Custom Code for Clean Core in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi @Ramjee_korada ,

the purpose of tier 2 is to cloud-enable not released APIs for ABAP cloud development in tier 1. After creating a custom wrapper you can release it for ABAP Cloud (switch the language version) and use in tier 1. The purpose of classic APIs for tier 3 is just to support your upgrade stability: if you still need to extend your classic developments in tier 3 you would rely on tier 3 Classic APIs. But the main development target is tier 1, and tier 2 is just supporting the cloud enablement of missing APIs for tier 1. The ATC checks for tier 2 and tier 3 Classic APIs will be also different.

Kind Regards,
Olga.
